ddls:
CREATE TABLE "workclasses" (
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each work class */,
  "workclass_name" TEXT /* Name of the work class (e.g., Self-emp-not-inc, Private) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the work class */,
  "is_federal_job" INTEGER /* Indicates if the work class is a federal job */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workclass_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "educations" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level (e.g., Bachelors, HS-grad) */,
  "education_num" INTEGER /* Number associated with the education level */,
  "degree_type" TEXT /* Type of degree (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, PhD) */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation (e.g., Exec-managerial, Handlers-cleaners) */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector of the occupation (e.g., Public, Private, Non-profit) */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the occupation (e.g., Healthcare, Finance, Technology) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship status */,
  "relationship_name" TEXT /* Name of the relationship status (e.g., Married-civ-spouse, Divorced) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the relationship status */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race */,
  "race_name" TEXT /* Name of the race (e.g., White, Black) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the race */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sexes" (
  "sex_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sex */,
  "sex_name" TEXT /* Name of the sex (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the sex */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sex_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country (e.g., United-States, Cuba) */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "gdp" TEXT /* GDP of the country */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_statuses" (
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the marital status (e.g., Married, Single) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the marital status */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "people_marital_education" (
  "people_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each person */,
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to marital_statuses */,
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to educations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("people_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "people" (
  "person_id" INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  "education_id" INTEGER,
  "workclass_id" INTEGER,
  "relationship_id" INTEGER,
  -- other columns...
  FOREIGN KEY ("education_id") REFERENCES "educations"("education_id"),
  FOREIGN KEY ("workclass_id") REFERENCES "workclasses"("workclass_id"),
  FOREIGN KEY ("relationship_id") REFERENCES "relationships"("relationship_id")
)
